game_name: Star Wars Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II

design_philosophy.core_loop: FPS combat with lightsaber and Force powers; linear level progression with objective-based advancement
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pursuing father's killer (Jerec) while discovering Force powers and moral choice between light/dark side paths
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming a Jedi knight; wielding lightsaber and Force powers; seeking revenge while embracing the Force
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces moral alignment through non-violent NPC interactions and Force power choices—light side requires restraint and pacifism toward innocent NPCs, while dark side embraces violence. Endings are locked behind these choices, creating meaningful divergence. Hybrid light/dark play is penalized.

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoint system; 21 discrete levels with objectives that must be completed before progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can allocate earned stars to Force powers between levels, creating strategic progression choices. Secret discovery grants bonus stars, rewarding exploration. Light/dark side choice determines available powers but is irreversible—stars cannot be reclaimed.

progression.upgrade_system: Star-based Force power allocation system. Players earn stars by completing levels; finding all secrets in a level grants one bonus star. Stars are allocated to enhance Force powers (light, dark, or neutral) between levels. Irreversible commitment to power choices.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Lightsaber and Force powers unlock after level 3. Additional Force powers become available as players progress and earn stars. Light/dark side endings locked behind player behavior (avoiding harm to NPCs vs. harming them) and power choice adherence.
replayability_hooks: Two distinct endings based on light/dark side alignment
Secret discovery and completionist star collection
Different Force power builds create varying playstyle approaches

world_building_techniques: Force spirit communication (Qu Rahn visits Kyle in vision)
NPC interaction and dialogue within levels
Environmental storytelling through level design and discovered objects (Morgan's workshop, data disk, droid WeeGee)
Expanded Universe setting (5 ABY, post-Return of the Jedi) anchors narrative in established Star Wars canon

notable_bosses: Jerec (primary antagonist—father's killer, Dark Jedi seeking Valley of the Jedi)

faction_indicators: Light side alignment: mercenary becomes Jedi protector, avoids killing innocents, seeks redemption
Dark side alignment: embrace of violent Force powers (choking, object throwing), harm to NPCs
Neutral Force powers available regardless of alignment choice (athletic enhancements)
danger_signals: Dark Jedi threat (Jerec and followers seeking Valley of the Jedi for power)
Betrayal by information broker droid 8t88 demonstrates deception within underworld
environmental_storytelling: Morgan's workshop contains family history and map location clues
Nar Shaddaa lower levels suggest underworld criminal infrastructure
Presence of Imperial stormtroopers and diverse enemy types (monsters, vehicles) indicates active conflict zones
WeeGee droid (family relic) provides continuity and emotional anchor

sources_used: wikipedia: Star Wars Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II
